scope.question: Use the retained PMC author-manuscript text snapshot for PMID 38956196 to propose a term-specific headache adverse-event result for the D+Q postmenopausal bone RCT. Keep the change bounded to the headache row in Extended Data Table 1, preserve blank-cell zero/not-reported ambiguity, and keep safety pooling blocked unless the existing schemas can support a defensible comparative effect.

inputs.summary: Coordinator job specification for the bounded D+Q bone headache adverse-event extraction pilot.
Repository operating rules for bounded extraction, source-snapshot provenance, candidate ledgers, review gates, and validation.
Schemas used to shape the final agent run, candidate change, result, synthesis group, and review-lane decisions.
Retained PMC author-manuscript source snapshot for PMID 38956196.
Full-text snapshot with normalized markdown and section index for the PMC author manuscript.
Retained article text and section index used to extract the Extended Data Table 1 headache row and safety-section context.
Scoped adverse-event outcome, existing mixed adverse-event term result, and safety synthesis boundary updated by this extraction refresh.

outputs.summary: Created the submitted headache adverse-event extraction candidate, added a full-text-extracted term-specific headache safety_event result with the D+Q 16/30 count, preserved the blank control-cell ambiguity, and updated the 20-week safety synthesis group to include the new result while keeping pooling blocked.

proposed_records.rationale: Tracks the bounded headache adverse-event extraction pilot and required review lanes.
Extracts the headache preferred-term row from Extended Data Table 1 as a descriptive safety_event result without converting the blank control cell to zero.
Adds the headache term-specific result to the safety synthesis boundary and records schema and zero/not-reported blockers that keep pooling blocked.

blocking_issues: The current result schema cannot directly encode adverse_event.preferred_term or adverse_event.event_specific_counts; the headache term and D+Q count are represented through valid result fields while the synthesis group records these structured-field blockers.

The Extended Data Table 1 control headache cell is blank under a table threshold for events reported in more than one participant, so no defensible control count, risk ratio, risk difference, or uncertainty was extracted.
next_actions: Move the submitted candidate into in_review only when review begins, then create active evidence_review records for extraction_fidelity, taxonomy_mapping, safety_limitations, and synthesis_boundary.
Extend the result schema for structured adverse-event preferred terms and event-specific arm counts before any comparative safety pooling attempt.
